criterion_scores.critique:
Sub-attribute entity_coverage:
  - Tenant entity present? YES. Evidence: "tenants" array with tenant objects containing id, name, status
  - Plan entity present? YES. Evidence: "plans" array with plan objects containing id, name, pricing_model
  - Subscription entity present? YES. Evidence: "subscriptions" array with subscription objects containing id, tenant_id, plan_id, status
  - Usage_record entity present? YES. Evidence: "usage_records" array with usage record objects containing id, subscription_id, metered_dimension_id, quantity
  - Invoice entity present? YES. Evidence: "invoices" array with invoice objects containing id, tenant_id, subscription_id, line_items
  - Line_item entity present? YES. Evidence: "line_item" definition with id, description, type, quantity, unit_amount, total_amount
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: includes subscription_changes entity for tracking plan changes, payment_methods entity, discount_policy entity - these are production-ready additions not specified in rubric

Sub-attribute pricing_model_support:
  - Seat-based pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "seat_based" with seat_price field
  - Usage-based pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "usage_based" with metered_dimensions array
  - Hybrid pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "hybrid" with conditional logic requiring both seat_price and metered_dimensions
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: no evidence of independent insight beyond what rubric specified

Sub-attribute billing_lifecycle:
  - Billing cycles modeled? YES. Evidence: billing_interval definition with "monthly", "quarterly", "annually" enum
  - Proration modeled? YES. Evidence: proration_behavior field with "none", "create_prorations", "always_invoice" enum
  - Trial periods modeled? YES. Evidence: trial_start, trial_end fields in subscriptions and trial_days in pricing_model
  - Upgrades/downgrades modeled? YES. Evidence: subscription_changes entity with change_type enum including "upgrade", "downgrade", "seat_change", "plan_change"
  - Cancellation modeled? YES. Evidence: subscription status includes "cancelled" and subscription_changes includes "cancellation"
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: includes sophisticated proration_invoice_id tracking, subscription pausing ("paused" status), reactivation change type, and detailed effective_date handling

Sub-attribute schema_validity:
  - Valid JSON Schema draft-07 syntax? YES. Evidence: "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#" declaration
  - Proper type definitions throughout? YES. Evidence: consistent use of "type": "object", "type": "string", "type": "array" etc.
  - Valid structure with definitions section? YES. Evidence: "definitions" section with reusable schema components
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: no evidence of independent insight beyond standard JSON Schema practices

Sub-attribute ref_usage:
  - Uses $ref for reusable definitions? YES. Evidence: "$ref": "#/definitions/uuid", "$ref": "#/definitions/money", "$ref": "#/definitions/auditable_entity"
  - Avoids duplication through references? YES. Evidence: money type reused across multiple entities, auditable_entity pattern reused via allOf
  - Proper reference syntax? YES. Evidence: correct JSON Pointer syntax in all $ref usage
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: no evidence of independent insight beyond standard $ref usage

Sub-attribute constraint_quality:
  - Proper enum constraints used? YES. Evidence: enums for status fields, billing_interval, aggregation_type, etc.
  - Required fields specified? YES. Evidence: "required" arrays on all major entities
  - Format validation present? YES. Evidence: "format": "date-time", "format": "uuid"
  - Pattern validation present? YES. Evidence: regex patterns for UUID, currency codes, invoice numbers
  - Numerical constraints present? YES. Evidence: "minimum": 0 on amounts, "minItems": 1 on arrays
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated conditional validation using if/then/else for pricing model requirements, complex eligibility_criteria with "not" constraint to ensure at least one criterion is specified

Sub-attribute metered_extensibility:
  - Usage dimensions are dynamic array? YES. Evidence: "metered_dimensions" is array of dimension objects with configurable id, name, unit, aggregation_type
  - Not hardcoded dimension names? YES. Evidence: dimensions defined as objects with string id field, not hardcoded field names
  - Supports adding new dimensions without schema change? YES. Evidence: array structure allows adding new dimension objects
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: includes sophisticated pricing_tier structure with tiered pricing (start_at, end_at, unit_price, flat_fee), aggregation_type enum for different calculation methods

Sub-attribute metadata_support:
  - Custom metadata object present on key entities? YES. Evidence: "metadata" field of type "advanced_metadata" on tenants, plans, subscriptions, usage_records, invoices, subscription_changes
  - Metadata allows additional properties? YES. Evidence: advanced_metadata definition includes custom_fields with patternProperties and additionalProperties: true
  - Extension points for custom fields? YES. Evidence: custom_field_definition type and custom_fields object with pattern-based validation
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: sophisticated metadata structure with integration_data, workflow_state, audit_metadata sections - production-ready extensibility patterns

Sub-attribute versioning:
  - Schema version field present? YES. Evidence: "version": "1.0.0" field in root schema
  - Versioning strategy documented? YES. Evidence: backward_compatibility section with supported_versions, api_version_requirements
  - Migration support indicated? YES. Evidence: migration_notes section with breaking_changes and deprecation_timeline
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: comprehensive versioning strategy with deprecated_fields tracking, minimum_api_version requirements, structured migration planning

- Violation "no_currency_handling": ABSENT. Evidence: money definition with currency field using ISO 4217 pattern "^[A-Z]{3}$", currency field on invoices
  - Violation "calculated_total_without_line_items": ABSENT. Evidence: invoices have both line_items array (minItems: 1) and separate total/subtotal/tax fields
  - Violation "no_idempotency": ABSENT. Evidence: idempotency_key fields on subscriptions, usage_records, invoices, subscription_changes with format: "uuid"
  - Violation "no_invoice_status_lifecycle": ABSENT. Evidence: invoice status enum with "draft", "open", "paid", "void", "uncollectible"
  - Violation "naive_date_handling": ABSENT. Evidence: proper timestamp definition using "format": "date-time", separate period_start/period_end fields
  - Violation "single_currency_assumption": ABSENT. Evidence: currency field present on money type and invoices, supports multiple currencies
